paper: Corporate and Other Laws  |  Master Test 01

steps.for: scheme 1 - s.16 provision, RD may direct change
scheme 2 - 3 months, ordinary resolution, 3 year window
scheme 1 - s.35(1) applied to Alok, no remedy
scheme 4 - reasonable ground only; fair representation + competence missing, half of 2
scheme 1 - Rule 2(1)(c)(iii), bank loan not a deposit

_notes: Reasoning lives on the REMARKS page. The copy keeps only marks and spot marks - the underlines under her wrong section numbers and the cross on her "Valid" conclusion.

remarks.title: Part I
lines: 11 of 15. Q3 and Q6 wrong, the other six correct. On Q3 you struck out a first
answer and wrote (B) beside it; the surviving answer is clear so it was marked as
written - but the key is (A), refund within 15 days.

remarks.title: Related party / wholly owned subsidiaries
lines: Not attempted. Question 1 is compulsory and this part carries 5 marks.
Needed: Sec 2(76)(viii) - a subsidiary of a holding company to which it is also a
subsidiary is a related party - but Notification G.S.R. 464(E) disapplies clause
(viii) to Sec 188 for a private company.

remarks.title: Company name / Trade Marks Act
lines: Earned: the provision, and 3 months, ordinary resolution and the 3 year window, all
produced correctly. Ground (ii) concluded correctly - the window is 3 years, not 2.
Not earned: ground (i) is never concluded. You needed to say the restriction covers
not only an existing company's name but also an application by a registered trade
mark proprietor, and that the name is verbatim identical to Mr X's mark. The
paragraph that would have done it was struck out. That is 2 marks.
Warning: Sec 16 is never named.

remarks.title: Expert liability for misstatement in prospectus
lines: Earned: Sec 35(1) applied to Mr Alok and the conclusion that he has no remedy - the
model's exact conclusion. Both half-mark exceptions are right too.
Half earned: point (iii) is one line. It needed the report to be a correct and fair
representation AND reasonable ground to believe the expert was competent.
Warning - wrong sections (underlined on the copy): you cite Sec 34 and Sec 447.
Civil liability is Sec 35; Sec 34 is criminal, Sec 447 is fraud. Marks given
because the reasoning and conclusion are correct.

remarks.title: Bonus shares
lines: Not attempted. Needed: the pre-requisites under Sec 63(2) - authorised by the
Articles, recommended by the Board and authorised in general meeting, no default on
deposits or debt securities or employees' dues, partly paid shares made fully paid,
and Rule 14 compliance. One mark each, max 5.

remarks.title: Deposits
lines: Earned: part (b) in full - the Rule 3(5) limit of 35% of paid-up capital, free
reserves and securities premium, and why 25% is wrong. Part (a)'s provision is right
too: a loan from a banking company is not a deposit.
Not earned: your conclusion in (a) contradicts your own reasoning. You prove the ABC
Bank loan is NOT a deposit, then write that Mr Taar's opinion "is Valid" (crossed on
the copy). His opinion was that it IS a deposit - so your analysis makes it NOT
correct. The law was right; the last line reversed it.

remarks.title: Service of documents
lines: Not attempted. Needed: Sec 20 - service on a company by registered post, speed post,
courier, leaving it at the registered office or prescribed electronic mode; and
Sec 20(2) for the Registrar or a member, with a member's right to a particular mode
on paying the fee fixed in the AGM.

remarks.title: Private placement
lines: Earned: the Rule 14(2) ceiling of 200 persons in aggregate in a financial year, and
both exclusions - QIBs AND ESOP employees. That is the substance of part (i).
Half earned: Sec 42(2)'s select group identified by the Board, only partly stated.
Not earned: the Explanation - the limit is reckoned separately for each kind of
security - and all of part (ii), which the answer never reaches.
Warning: you open "the statement is invalid". It is PARTLY correct - only the ESOP
claim is wrong. Say which part fails rather than rejecting all of it.

rating.name: Introduction Competence
rating.note: You open with the provision every time, which is right. Weak point: no section number in the opening line.

rating.name: Content Coverage
rating.note: Your weakest area. Q1(a), Q2(a) and Q3(a) are blank - 15 marks with nothing written at all.

rating.name: Conclusion Competence
rating.note: You always write a conclusion - a good habit. But Q2(b) contradicts your own reasoning, Q3(b) overstates.

rating.name: Presentation & Structure
rating.note: Your best area. Pointwise, numbered, well spaced, conclusions underlined. Easy to follow. Keep this.

rating.name: Language Proficiency
rating.note: Clear, correct legal language and grammar. Watch spelling - "propreitor", "resonable", "relevent".

feedback.head: WHAT YOU DID WELL
lines: Your provisions are strong. In Q1(b) you wrote 3 months, ordinary resolution
and 3 years on your own. All correct.
In Q1(c) your final answer is the same as the model answer. "No remedy" is right.
You know the 35% deposit rule well.

feedback.head: FIX THESE TWO HABITS
lines: *1.  Your last line does not match your own working.
In Q2(b)(a) you proved the bank loan is not a deposit. Then you wrote that
Mr Taar's opinion "is Valid". His opinion was the opposite of what you proved.
Read the question once more before you write the conclusion.
*2.  Write the section number.
You wrote Sec 34 and Sec 447 where Sec 35 applies. You did not write Sec 16
anywhere. No marks were cut this time. But in the exam the section number is
what shows the examiner you know the provision.

feedback.head: YOUR BIGGEST LOSS IS BLANK SPACE, NOT WRONG ANSWERS
lines: Q1(a), Q2(a) and Q3(a) have nothing written at all - that is 15 marks.
Q3(b) stops in the middle and part (ii) is never reached.
*Out of the 35 marks you attempted, you scored 24.5. That is 70%.
So your law is not the problem - blank space is. If you had written even half
of those three questions, your total would look completely different.
If time is short, write the provision and the conclusion in two lines instead
of leaving the page empty. Never leave a compulsory question.

footer: If you want to go deeper on any of this, you can connect with an SME.
